blocks.content: Between second and third period. Jonathan had gone ahead — someone wanted to show him where the weight room was.
That was when Daniel Snyder found him.
Daniel was a sophomore. That one year shouldn't have made a difference. It did.
It wasn't a question. Larry kept walking.
He stepped in front of him. Not aggressive enough to start a fight. Just enough to be a wall.
He repeated it like he was testing whether it was worth remembering.
There it was. Not who Larry was. Who he came with.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Cause Michaels is that dude. You though?
He looked Larry up and down. Unhurried. Like he had all morning.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: I don't know what you are yet.
